metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 J
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Employer, breeder, applicant, licensee, and legal-holder roles remain distinct; historical rights were not reassigned to later employers, and no external communication was made.

profiles.ownershipType: independent breeder and owner-managed private fruit company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Spanish fruit breeder and sole administrator of Frutos Canela S.L. MAPA filings name Frutos Canela as applicant and Nemesio Gomez Martin as breeder for Lozana, Gustosa, Tepis, Kuton, Fervo, Salada, and Dades, but the Hirpa filing reverses those roles and names him personally as applicant.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Founder-dependent operation. The commercial register names Nemesio Gomez Martin as sole administrator of Frutos Canela, and no public successor or separate breeding bench was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split Hirpa from the company-applicant records unless a later assignment proves common title. The 2023 strawberry applications were later renamed Llosana and Veleza. No official Frutos Canela website, cultivar-licensing contact, public business email, or public business phone was found.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Named inventor in the DrisRasp portfolio. The cited patent was filed by and assigned to Driscoll's, Inc.; no personal ownership is shown.
profiles.headquarters: Driscoll's corporate headquarters, Watsonville, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Part of a multi-inventor corporate raspberry program with institutional continuity; no public source established his current title or a personal successor role.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Repeated DrisRasp inventorship and an assignment to Driscoll's support corporate affiliation, but the public sources reviewed do not prove a current job title.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll the cited DrisRasp rights into Driscoll's, not the individual. Current employment and title remain unconfirmed, so the Driscoll's relationship is recorded as a candidate parent rather than a current personnel claim.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The abbreviated name resolves to Marco Stefanini through the IASMA ECO cultivar and patent record. Fondazione Edmund Mach is the cited patent assignee and institutional breeding organization; Stefanini is the inventor and program contact, not the personal rightsholder.
profiles.parentCompany: Fondazione Edmund Mach - Research and Innovation Centre
profiles.leadershipSummary: Marco Stefanini leads or is the named contact for a visible institutional grape physiology and breeding unit, providing a broader research bench beyond one inventor.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Grapevine Physiology and Breeding research-unit lead/contact
profiles.webResearchNotes: The IASMA ECO patent resolves M. Stefanini to Marco Stefanini. Preserve Fondazione Edmund Mach as assignee for those patents; later national or PBR records should retain the holder named in each filing rather than inheriting ownership solely from his current affiliation.

profiles.ownershipType: private orchard breeder working through multiple corporate rightsholders
profiles.ownershipSummary: Australian fruit breeder associated with separate title chains. Cherry Royale Pty Ltd is applicant or assignee for Nadia and Rullo Special, while Fivefifty5 Super Foods Pty Ltd is assignee for later AJOP20 and JT1 records.
profiles.leadershipSummary: A long-running orchard-breeding practice is visible, but no official public source identified a successor or a durable breeding bench across the two rightsholder companies.

webResearchSources.label: IP Australia Rullo Special PBR journal

profiles.webResearchNotes: Create separate ownership records for Cherry Royale and Fivefifty5. Do not assign Nadia or Rullo Special to the later Fivefifty5 assignee. No official breeder/company website or public cultivar-licensing contact was found.

profiles.ownershipType: cooperative research employee and strategic stone-fruit breeding operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: MAS.PES breeder and technical researcher. A government protection list names CRPV Soc. Coop. as applicant for Nirosa 1 and Nirosa 2; Ri.Nova documents that CRPV changed its legal name to Ri.Nova after the 2021 Alimos incorporation. Foschi is not shown as personal titleholder.
profiles.parentCompany: Ri.Nova Soc. Coop. (formerly CRPV Soc. Coop.)
profiles.leadershipSummary: The MAS.PES program has a visible institutional bench across Ri.Nova, universities, producer groups, and nurseries. Ri.Nova's organization chart also places Foschi within a larger fruit-sector team.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve CRPV as the applicant name at the 2019 filing date and Ri.Nova as the documented later name of that legal organization. Cultivar-level co-applicants, territorial grants, and licenses still require file-by-file review; current employment alone is not ownership proof.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Takayoshi Yamane, a NARO fruit-tree researcher and co-breeder. The supplied Sakuhime, Tsukikagami, and related institutional cultivars are NARO program outputs; no personal title evidence was found.
profiles.parentCompany: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization (NARO)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Active senior researcher in a multi-author national fruit breeding program. NARO's institutional breeding and licensing functions provide continuity beyond the individual.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Employment history includes Hiroshima prefectural research before NARO, but that earlier employer should not receive title to later NARO cultivars. Route rights inquiries to NARO, not the individual.

profiles.ownershipSummary: JKI scientist and apple breeder. The Pia41 patent names Julius Kuhn-Institut, Federal Research Centre for Cultivated Plants, as assignee; Peil is the inventor rather than a personal rightsholder.
profiles.parentCompany: Julius Kuhn-Institut (JKI), Federal Research Centre for Cultivated Plants
profiles.leadershipSummary: Embedded in JKI's Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit with an institutional team and commercialization partners; no individual successor to Peil was publicly designated.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat JKI as the cited institutional rightsholder and Peil as breeder/inventor. Commercialization partners such as Herbert Knuppen are licensing channels, not evidence of underlying ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Director of Breeding at Edward Vinson Ltd. The cited strawberry patents name Edward Vinson Ltd as assignee; Clarkson is inventor and program leader, not personal rightsholder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Edward Vinson describes a core multi-crop breeding team and a program operating since 1986. That team provides operating continuity, although no named successor to Clarkson was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Historical Vitacress employment is career history, not evidence that Vitacress owns the supplied Edward Vinson cultivar rights. Route current inquiries to Edward Vinson rather than the individual.

profiles.ownershipType: former institutional breeder, current private-company director, and individual rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical HortResearch raspberry patents were assigned to The Horticulture and Food Research Institute of New Zealand Ltd. The later Shekinah Tahi patent names Harvey Hall as inventor and lists an individual assignee, so those rights cannot be rolled into either the old institute or Shekinah Berries Limited without further assignment evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Current company registration: Rotorua, Bay of Plenty, New Zealand
profiles.leadershipSummary: Shekinah Berries' public company record names Harvey and Robyn Hall as directors and identifies Robyn Hall and David Mundy alongside Harvey as shareholders. No named breeding successor was found.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Current director/shareholder affiliation only. The Shekinah Tahi patent remains individually assigned, and historical HortResearch rights remain with their historical institutional chain.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split historical HortResearch/PFR records from current individual and company-affiliation records. Do not assign Karaka Black, Wakefield, NR7, or other institutional-era rights to Shekinah Berries. No public cultivar-licensing email or phone was found.

profiles.ownershipType: independent fruit farmer, breeder, and individual rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Masumi Shiohara of Shiojiri, Nagano. Official MAFF records name him as both breeder and registered rightsholder for Rabery, Labered, Syawase, and Vellum Red.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Public biographical material describes a return to the family orchard, but no named successor, co-owner, or formal breeding bench was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The official records support direct individual title. The listed email is a public professional inquiry address, not a cultivar-specific licensing channel; no trademark claim was located.

profiles.ownershipSummary: UC Davis-associated inventor on the HBOK peach-rootstock series. The cited patent assignments divide title between The Regents of the University of California and the United States as represented by the Secretary of Agriculture; Almehdi has no personal title in the reviewed records.
profiles.headquarters: University of California, Davis, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The HBOK work was produced by a multi-institution team, and UC Davis's Plant Variety Licensing Program and USDA ownership provide continuity. Almehdi's current role was not established.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: UC publications and the Regents assignment support historical UC Davis affiliation, but no current job title was found.
profiles.primaryContactName: UC Davis Plant Variety Licensing Program
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat the HBOK records as joint institutional rights, not personal assets. The Google Patents normalized assignee label is imprecise for the Regents, so the recorded assignment events control the ownership summary.

profiles.ownershipType: prefectural public-research breeder and national-program collaborator
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Toshiharu Ozawa, whose name appears in both Yamanashi prefectural and NARO grape teams. Yamanashi Prefecture is the official rightsholder for cited prefectural cultivars, while NARO controls licensing for NARO-bred cultivars such as the national grape-program records; neither chain is personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station, Yamanashi, Japan; separate NARO fruit-program locations in Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Both the Yamanashi station and NARO maintain active breeding departments and institutional licensing functions. Ozawa's current personal role and a named successor were not established.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Research publications support Ozawa's Yamanashi station affiliation. This affiliation does not transfer title to NARO-owned collaborative cultivars.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Create separate Yamanashi Prefecture and NARO ownership records. Co-breeding credits do not merge the two public rightsholders, and current station affiliation must not be used to reassign NARO rights.

profiles.ownershipType: public agricultural research-station director and named breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact transliteration appears as an author of Konotopska, Nizhynska, Slobozhanska, and Maliuk in the Ukrainian state variety bulletin. Public research records tie him to the Sumy Horticultural Research Station, but the reviewed sources do not identify the legal PBR holder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2020 public election record identifies Viacheslav Filov as station director, and the variety records name Chukhil and Chyzh as co-breeders. Current leadership, succession, and operating capacity were not verified.
profiles.candidateParent: Sumy Horticultural Research Station of the Institute of Horticulture, NAAS of Ukraine
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The national research-project record supports institutional affiliation and authorship, but does not prove that the station holds the cultivar rights.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Identity and station affiliation are supported despite transliteration variants, but authorship is not legal title. Do not assign the four rootstock rights to either Filov or the station until a holder or assignment record is located. The email and phone are public institutional contacts from an agricultural directory.

profiles.ownershipType: public agricultural research scientist and apple breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Kazuma Okada of NARO's fruit-tree and tea research institute. NARO cultivar papers identify him within the apple breeding teams, and NARO manages rights and licensing for its registered varieties; no personal title was found.
profiles.parentCompany: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization (NARO)
profiles.headquarters: NARO apple breeding program, Morioka, Iwate, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Active NARO researcher working in broad multi-author apple teams. The institution's breeding and licensing functions provide a durable bench beyond the individual.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat the supplied apple records as institutional NARO outputs and route licensing to NARO. Inventorship and current employment do not create personal ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: identity unresolved across public-university grape-breeding records
profiles.ownershipSummary: University of Pecs sources identify Dr. Pal Kozma Jr. as the breeder who led the Pecs resistant-grape program, but the supplied exact name also aggregates a 2001 Georgikon 28 record with later Pecs applications. The name alone does not prove that those generations, institutions, or titleholders are the same.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Pecs program has institutional continuity: its gene bank records Kozma's leadership, and later public material identifies Peter Teszlak as research director. That bench does not resolve the separate Georgikon identity or title chain.
profiles.candidateParent: University of Pecs Research Institute for Viticulture and Oenology
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institute sources support the later Pecs breeding affiliation and program leadership, but the reviewed pages do not prove cultivar-level title or connect the 2001 Georgikon record to the same person.
profiles.primaryContactName: University of Pecs Research Institute for Viticulture and Oenology

webResearchSources.label: University of Pecs gene bank history

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split Georgikon 28 from the later Pecs portfolio until the exact Pal Kozma generation and each applicant/rightsholder are confirmed. Pecs affiliation is supported for the later program, but employer affiliation is not title evidence and no trademark claim was located.
